2604.24429.title: A Multi-Dimensional Audit of Politically Aligned Large Language Models
2604.24429.paper_summary: As the application of Large Language Models (LLMs) spreads across various industries, there are increasing concerns about the potential for their misuse, especially in sensitive areas such as political discourse. Deliberately aligning LLMs with specific political ideologies, through prompt engineering or fine-tuning techniques, can be advantageous in use cases such as political campaigns, but requires careful consideration due to heightened risks of performance degradation, misinformation, or increased biased behavior. In this work, we propose a multi-dimensional framework inspired by Habermas' Theory of Communicative Action to audit politically aligned language models across four dimensions: effectiveness, fairness, truthfulness, and persuasiveness using automated, quantitative metrics. Applying this to nine popular LLMs aligned via fine-tuning or role-playing revealed consistent trade-offs: while larger models tend to be more effective at role-playing political ideologies and truthful in their responses, they were also less fair, exhibiting higher levels of bias in the form of angry and toxic language towards people of different ideologies. Fine-tuned models exhibited lower bias and more effective alignment than the corresponding role-playing models, but also saw a decline in performance reasoning tasks and an increase in hallucinations. Overall, all of the models tested exhibited some deficiency in at least one of the four metrics, highlighting the need for more balanced and robust alignment strategies. Ultimately, this work aims to ensure politically-aligned LLMs generate legitimate, harmless arguments, offering a framework to evaluate the responsible political alignment of these models.
2604.24429.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）在各行各業的應用擴展，人們對其潛在誤用的擔憂日益增加，特別是在政治話語等敏感領域。故意通過提示工程或微調技術使LLMs與特定政治意識形態對齊，在政治運動等用例中可能是有利的，但由於性能下降、錯誤信息或偏見行為增加的風險加大，這需要謹慎考量。在本研究中，我們提出了一個多維框架，靈感來自哈貝馬斯的交往行動理論，以自動化的定量指標來審核在四個維度上與政治對齊的語言模型：有效性、公平性、真實性和說服力。將這一框架應用於九個通過微調或角色扮演對齊的流行LLMs，揭示了一致的權衡：雖然較大的模型在角色扮演政治意識形態和回答中較為真實，但它們的公平性較低，對不同意識形態的人表現出更高程度的偏見，以憤怒和有毒的語言表達。微調模型的偏見較低，對齊效果比相應的角色扮演模型更有效，但在推理任務中的性能有所下降，並且幻覺現象增加。總體而言，所有測試的模型在至少一個四個指標中均顯示出某種不足，突顯了需要更平衡和穩健的對齊策略。最終，本研究旨在確保政治對齊的LLMs生成合法、無害的論點，提供一個評估這些模型負責任的政治對齊的框架。
2604.24429.author: Lisa Korver et.al.
2604.24429.authors: Lisa Korver, Mohamed Mostagir, Sherief Reda
